mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in over 65 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which amplifies corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive rank IC greater than 0.04 over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which supports corporate margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55 percent of observations when industrial production growth exceeds its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 3.0 percent annually forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, which increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening without immediate recessionary signals, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a rank IC below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window or fails to outperform single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend levels confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which directly translates into higher corporate profit forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no statistical significance or produce negative alpha when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals that restrictive financial conditions are successfully suppressing real output, which triggers recession fears and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 65 percent of observations when industrial production declines during tightening periods.
